Scenario.title: Healthcare Data Manipulation via Phishing and Exploitation
Scenario.overview: Healthcare and Public Health Simple A cybercriminal group leverages phishing attacks to compromise healthcare staff, exploits unpatched web portal vulnerabilities, and manipulates electronic health records to disrupt operations and extort ransom.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Application Log, OS Process
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Web, Phishing, Delivery Method, Application Log Content, Data Manipulation

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2019-3568 (CWE-120): Buffer overflow vulnerability in WhatsApp VOIP stack allowing remote code execution via crafted RTCP packets
stage.description: The attacker sends spear-phishing emails impersonating legitimate healthcare vendors, embedding malicious hyperlinks in PDF attachments. When staff click the link, they are redirected to a spoofed login page that captures credentials, granting access to internal systems.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2010-3904 (CWE-200): Linux Kernel RDS protocol input validation flaw enabling privilege escalation via crafted system calls
stage.description: Using stolen credentials, the attacker exploits an unpatched vulnerability in the hospital's web portal application logs. By injecting malformed data into log entries, they trigger a buffer overflow, executing arbitrary code to establish a command-and-control (C2) connection.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker deploys malware to alter critical electronic health records (EHRs), such as modifying medication dosages and falsifying patient diagnoses. This disruption forces hospitals to halt operations temporarily, demanding cryptocurrency ransom to restore data integrity.

Scenario.summary: A low-skill threat group compromises healthcare organizations through phishing, exploits known vulnerabilities in public-facing applications, and corrupts EHRs to extort ransom, demonstrating a straightforward yet effective attack chain targeting operational continuity.
